name: pi-no-extensions
subject: pi --no-extensions
notes: Conservative self-declaration for bare pi without extensions or an OpenProse adapter. Raw chat is available, but there is no first-class subagent primitive exposed in this mode, so any non-trivial OpenProse execution profile is blocked immediately.

subagents.notes: No first-class subagent primitive when extensions are disabled.

file-io.notes: Local file read/write/edit support remains available in the base harness.

tool-exec.notes: Shell command execution remains available in the base harness.

ask-user.notes: The harness can still ask the human user for clarification through the main chat channel.

core.reason: No first-class subagent primitive in --no-extensions mode, and the required protocol capabilities are not exposed through a stable OpenProse interface.

delegation.reason: Depends on Core and delegation is unsupported.

persistence.reason: Depends on Core and no OpenProse-shaped persistence interface is declared.

interaction.reason: Depends on Core. ask-user exists via the main chat channel, but the profile still requires Core.

tests.reason: Depends on Core and kind:test execution is unsupported.

resume.reason: Depends on Core and OpenProse resume semantics are unsupported.

path_to_conformance: Expose a first-class subagent interface and add an adapter that turns native file I/O + tool execution into stable OpenProse workspace/bindings/state/error protocol surfaces. Without subagents, Core profile conformance is blocked immediately for multi-service programs.
